message:
fix(freeze): Support nested recursive calls with tail_call in trampoline

The Ackermann function uses nested recursive calls like:
  return ack(m - 1, ack(m, n - 1));

This generates bytecode with multiple tail_call instructions in the
same block. The previous code only detected recursive calls when
pending_self_call was set by get_var, but after the first recursive
call was emitted, pending_self_call was reset to false, causing the
second tail_call to hit the unsupported opcode path.

Changes:
- tail_call in self-recursive functions is now ALWAYS treated as a
  recursive call, regardless of pending_self_call state
- This handles nested/chained recursive calls correctly

Test results:
  ack(2,3) = 9 ✓
  ack(3,3) = 61 ✓
  ack(3,4) = 125 ✓
